steps.title: Find the slope
steps.content:
Using the two given points $(0, 3)$ and $(1, -4)$:
$$m = \frac{-4 - 3}{1 - 0} = \frac{-7}{1} = -7$$

steps.title: Identify the y-intercept
steps.content:
The point $(0, 3)$ has $x = 0$, so the y-intercept is $b = 3$. The line in slope-intercept form is:
$$y = -7x + 3$$

steps.title: Evaluate each choice
steps.content:
• $y = 7x + 3$: slope $+7$, wrong sign; eliminate.
  • $y = -7x + 3$: slope $-7$, intercept $3$; matches.
  • $y = x + 3$: slope $1$, wrong; eliminate.
  • $y = -x + 3$: slope $-1$, wrong; eliminate.
That confirms B is correct.
